summary.what: Add readable entry panel for timbers show and log --dry-run
summary.why: Laura (a timbers user) couldn't scan log output: output.KeyValue rendered fields with no key alignment and no value wrapping, so long Why/How/Notes dumped as one soft-wrapped line and fields bled together. Chose a terse-default + on-demand-panel design over making the panel the default log output: agents call 'timbers log' without --json, so a default box would cost context tokens on every commit for no agent benefit. Dropped the originally-proposed --preview flag because 'timbers show --latest' already renders the same panel for free, making a 'preview that also writes' redundant and semantically odd.
summary.how: Added shared output.FieldsBox renderer that finally wires up the previously-unused output.Box helper (rounded border at TTY, borderless when piped); keys align to a common column, values wrap under the value column with a hanging indent. Terminal width via charmbracelet/x/term with 80-col fallback. Field assembly leads with substance and trails bookkeeping; show keeps ID as title, dry-run puts ID/Anchor at bottom. Folded in two bug fixes: dry-run dropped Notes; diffstat was formatted differently in show vs dry-run.

notes: Key fork: panel-by-default vs terse-default. Terse won on agent-token cost (agents log without --json). The --preview flag from the original handoff was cut once we saw show --latest covers the same need — fewer flags, no 'preview writes' weirdness. Wide-rune wrapping (byte-len vs display-width) deferred to timbers-qbm.
